zotero.abstractNote: We propose a framework for learning causal generative models of scenes. Our approach is based on the idea that a scene can be decomposed into a set of independent causal factors, which we model as a collection of experts. Each expert is responsible for generating a specific part of the scene, and the final scene is formed by a competition between these experts. We show that this framework can learn to disentangle causal factors from raw images and that it can be used for causal reasoning and intervention.
